frame_version: v5 -- Python-only, regex_best_effort dropped as unreliable (supersedes v4, commit b740919)
frame_status_note: A systematic audit (GG-directed, after 4 confirmed Python-extraction bugs and an implausibly clean v4 result) found the generic regex fallback used for ALL non-Python servers ('regex_best_effort' method) pulls in massive noise: template literal placeholders (e.g. '${tool.name}'), raw parameter names, MCP meta-concept labels ('List Tools', 'Read Resource'), HTTP header names ('X-Request-ID'), UI category labels ('E-commerce', 'Social Media'), and sample data unrelated to tool names (e.g. an org-chart generator's example 'CEO'/'CTO'/'Dev Manager' inputs), mixed indiscriminately with real tool names. This is a SYSTEMIC reliability problem with blind name/description-proximity regex matching over arbitrary non-Python source text -- not a fixable one-line patch like the 4 Python-specific bugs (those were narrow, well-defined gaps in a STRUCTURAL AST parser; this is the fundamental limit of an UNSTRUCTURED text-proximity heuristic). GG directed: drop ALL regex_best_effort-extracted servers from the scoreable frame rather than attempt further regex tightening (open-ended, uncertain payoff) or a full non-Python AST parser (large new engineering scope).
methodological_limitation: This frame is now Python-only (all 10 scoreable servers use one of: 'ast' -- AST decorator/kwarg parsing, the most reliable method; 'tool_constructor_python' -- a precise Tool(name=..., description=...) constructor-call regex anchored on the literal 'Tool' identifier; 'regex_fallback_on_python' -- the same generic proximity regex, but scoped to .py files only and, for the one server using it (LycheeMem), independently verified clean against source this session). The pre-registration's sampling_frame.filters.languages explicitly states 'ALL languages included... Language is NOT an exclusion criterion' -- this frame now violates that criterion for RELIABILITY reasons, not an arbitrary choice, and must be disclosed as a further amendment (see exp1_pre_registration.json). Extending trustworthy mechanical extraction to TypeScript/JavaScript/Go/Rust would require real per-language AST parsers, not regex heuristics -- explicitly out of scope for this pass.
predecessor_frame_note: v4 (commit b740919) superseded -- kept for audit trail.
discovery_source: GitHub topic:mcp-server, 500 repos fetched (unchanged from v1/v2 -- same 30 structurally-vetted candidates, re-scored not re-discovered)
vetting_method_note: Each candidate was screened by an independent research pass against two criteria GG specified: (a) is_focused_mcp_server -- primary purpose is exposing a FIXED MCP tool catalog, not a general platform/framework/aggregator/gateway/SDK-for-building-servers with MCP as an incidental feature; (b) approx_tool_count >= 5, so a confusable family (2+ similarly-named/purposed tools) can plausibly exist. ~55 candidates were screened across the 500-repo discovery pool; 30 passed. A previously-unanticipated failure mode was found and excluded throughout: aggregators/gateways/proxies that dynamically route to OTHER MCP servers rather than exposing their own fixed catalog (e.g. metatool-ai/metamcp, tadata-org/fastapi_mcp) -- these have no stable docstring surface to mirror.

doc_density_methodology.scorer: agentgauge/exp1_doc_density.py compute_doc_density() -- 30% description length, 25% description presence, 25% non-echo descriptions, 20% param-description coverage
doc_density_methodology.extractor: agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py -- deterministic, no LLM/agent: git clone --depth 1, static parsing (Python AST decorator + imperative add_tool() + JSON-literal fallback; regex proximity for other languages)
doc_density_methodology.tiering: assign_tiers() -- empirical terciles (p33/p67) of composite_score WITHIN the 23 successfully-scored fresh candidates. Tier names (well_documented/thin/near_empty) are RELATIVE to this sample, not absolute doc-quality bands -- see honest_caveat below.

reportable_finding: Across the 10 Python servers with trustworthy mechanical extraction, the minimum description coverage is 57.1% -- still a strong documented-skew signal, but on a much smaller, single-language sample than originally targeted. This should be reported as a LOWER-CONFIDENCE, narrower-scope finding (Python MCP servers on GitHub specifically) rather than the general 'public MCP servers' claim the pre-reg originally targeted, until non-Python extraction is made trustworthy.
next_step: Awaiting GG ratification of this Python-only frame (N=10) before any contested-task authoring or Arm A/B trials. Given N=10 is far below the pre-registered target (28) and the sample is now single-language, GG should decide whether to proceed with EXP-1 at this reduced scope, invest in real non-Python AST parsers first, or reconsider the experiment's scope.
